title: achillea moschata subsp. morisiana
intro: Achillea moschata subsp. morisiana, also known as Yarrow, is a perennial plant species in the sunflower family that is native to the Mediterranean region. The plant has been used in traditional medicine for its antiseptic, anti-inflammatory, and antispasmodic properties, which can be attributed to its essential oil composition. The health benefits of Yarrow include reducing menstrual cramps, relieving digestive issues, and aiding in wound healing due to its antiseptic properties. Yarrow is often used in herbal preparations such as teas, tinctures, and infusions, which can be consumed orally or applied topically to the skin for its medicinal benefits.
